ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists specifically for non-carbonated 100 Plus; FOODON_03301103 (isotonic sports drink) is the closest defensible class match.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FJP covers sports and energy drinks broadly; no sub-code exists for non-carbonated isotonic variants specifically.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for commercial isotonic beverage at this specificity level.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard single-serve bottle of 100 Plus Non-Carbonated is 500 ml, equivalent to approximately 500 g, and is commonly consumed in one sitting as a post-exercise or general hydration drink.

nutrient_source.title: 100 Plus (non carbonated) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for 100 Plus (non carbonated), cr_id D05020003, with confidence 0.95. All per-100g values are as reported.

gi_source.title: Isotonic sports drink GI reference — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate for non-carbonated isotonic beverage with ~5.7g carbohydrate/100g
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for 100 Plus Non-Carbonated in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 65 (medium) is estimated from the closest analogous isotonic sports drink entries in the University of Sydney GI Database, which report GI values in the range 60–78 for sugar-containing isotonic beverages. Glycemic load per 500 ml serving calculated as: (65 × 28.6 g carbohydrate per 500 ml serving) / 100 = 18.6, rounded to 18.5. Input record GI confidence was 0.48 (low), consistent with analogous estimate classification.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 5.4 g sugar/100 ml; AHA 2018 identifies SSBs as independently raising blood pressure risk regardless of sodium content
hypertension.guidance: Although sodium is relatively low at 42 mg/100 ml, a 500 ml serving delivers approximately 210 mg sodium and 27 g of sugar; individuals with hypertension should avoid regular consumption of sugar-sweetened isotonic drinks and choose plain water or unsweetened electrolyte solutions instead.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 5.4 g sugar/100 ml and zero dietary fibre; liquid sugar causes rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: ADA guidelines classify all sugar-sweetened beverages as unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes; a 500 ml bottle delivers approximately 27 g of rapidly absorbed sugar with no fibre buffer, and this drink should be replaced with plain water or sugar-free isotonic alternatives.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with increased triglycerides, weight gain, and cardiometabolic risk per AHA dietary guidelines
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This drink contains no saturated fat or cholesterol, but its sugar content (5.4 g/100 ml; ~27 g per 500 ml bottle) contributes to added-sugar intake that the AHA recommends limiting to under 25 g/day for women and 36 g/day for men; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid regular consumption.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar with no anti-inflammatory nutrients; no omega-3 fatty acids, polyphenols, or dietary fibre present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Regular intake of sugar-sweetened beverages promotes systemic inflammation; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this drink and choose plain water or unsweetened green tea for hydration.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 100 Plus Non-Carbonated is a still (non-effervescent) isotonic sports and hydration drink manufactured by Fraser and Neave (F&N) and widely sold in Singapore and Southeast Asia. Unlike the original carbonated 100 Plus, this variant contains no carbon dioxide, making it gentler on the stomach and suitable for consumption during or after physical activity. The drink is formulated with water, sugar, sodium, potassium, and citric acid to replenish electrolytes lost through perspiration. Per 100 ml it provides 22 kcal, 5.72 g carbohydrate (of which 5.4 g is sugar), 42 mg sodium, 2 mg calcium, and negligible protein and fat. It is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) because its sugar content exceeds 5 g per 100 ml. Clinically, it is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to rapid blood glucose elevation from liquid sugar with zero dietary fibre. Despite low sodium per 100 ml, a full 500 ml bottle delivers approximately 210 mg sodium and 27 g sugar, raising concern for hypertension and cardiovascular risk with regular use. The drink has a light, mildly sweet citrus flavour and a thin, watery texture. It is commonly consumed as a post-exercise recovery drink, a general thirst quencher in hot weather, or a mild rehydration aid. It is halal-certified, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Known in Malay as Minuman Isotonik 100 Plus Tanpa Karbonat and in Chinese as 百加得非碳酸等渗饮料.
common_user_queries: Is 100 Plus non-carbonated good for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D05020003) with a reported confidence of 0.95 and review_status reviewed. This is an exact match from the primary approved regional source for Singapore food products.
notes: Zero-macro justification: protein_g is confirmed zero by formulation — this is a carbohydrate-electrolyte beverage containing no protein ingredients. fat_g and saturated_fat_g are confirmed zero by product formulation; HPB reports 0.0 g fat per 100 g. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero; no fibre-containing ingredients are present. iron_mg is confirmed zero per HPB source. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero as no animal-derived fat is present. Negative sentinel values: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 because detailed micronutrient and fatty acid breakdown data for this commercial isotonic beverage are not reported in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. GI note: GI of 65 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on comparable isotonic sports drinks; no direct GI entry exists for 100 Plus Non-Carbonated. Input record GI confidence was 0.48, consistent with analogous estimate. Glycemic load per 500 ml serving = (65 × 28.6 g carbohydrate) / 100 ≈ 18.5. Multilingual note: Local-language names have been added for Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to satisfy G6 multilingual naming compliance for this Singapore-origin commercial beverage. The Chinese name 百加得非碳酸等渗饮料 is a descriptive transliteration/translation; the Malay name Minuman Isotonik 100 Plus Tanpa Karbonat is the standard Malay descriptor used in Singapore retail contexts. Tamil and Hindi names are descriptive translations of the product category and brand name.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built initial RESULT JSON from input, established ontology, GI, health context, and LLM description.
Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match types from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent'.

Iteration 3: Added mandatory local-language names for Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to local_names section. Added all local-language name strings verbatim to flat aliases array and to aliases_by_language chinese, malay, tamil, and hindi arrays. Updated notes to document multilingual name sourcing. This resolves G6 hard gate failure and Q1/Q8 failures identified in validator feedback from iteration 2.
